records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Missing Person
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5710 - Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman narrated from Ibn Awn from Mu'awiyah ibn Wahb from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a man who had a rightful claim over another person but lost contact with him. He did not know where to search for him, whether he was alive or dead, and was unaware of any heir, lineage, or child related to him.
Abu Abdullah (as) said: "He should continue searching for him."
The man said: "It has been a long time; can he give it in charity?"
Imam (as) replied: "He should continue searching."
records.arabicText: 5710 - وَ رَوَى يُونُسُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ عَنِ اِبْنِ عَوْنٍ عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ وَهْبٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ كَانَ لَهُ عَلَى رَجُلٍ حَقٌّ فَفَقَدَهُ وَ لاَ يَدْرِي أَيْنَ يَطْلُبُهُ وَ لاَ يَدْرِي أَ حَيٌّ هُوَ أَمْ مَيِّتٌ وَ لاَ يَعْرِفُ لَهُ وَارِثاً وَ لاَ نَسَباً وَ لاَ وَلَداً فَقَالَ "يَطْلُبُ" قَالَ إِنَّ ذَلِكَ قَدْ طَالَ عَلَيْهِ فَيَتَصَدَّقُ بِهِ قَالَ "يَطْلُبُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5710 - Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman narrated from Ibn Awn from Mu'awiyah ibn Wahb from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a man who had a rightful claim over another person but lost contact with him. He did not know where to search for him, whether he was alive or dead, and was unaware of any heir, lineage, or child related to him.
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Abu Abdullah (as) said: "He should continue searching for him."
The man said: "It has been a long time; can he give it in charity?"
Imam (as) replied: "He should continue searching."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Missing Person
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5711 - It has also been narrated in another report:
"If you do not find an heir for him, and Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, knows that you have made every effort, then give it in charity on his behalf."
records.arabicText: 5711 - وَ قَدْ رُوِيَ فِي هَذَا خَبَرٌ آخَرُ : "إِنْ لَمْ تَجِدْ لَهُ وَارِثاً وَ عَرَفَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مِنْكَ اَلْجَهْدَ فَتَصَدَّقْ بِهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5711 - It has also been narrated in another report:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you do not find an heir for him, and Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, knows that you have made every effort, then give it in charity on his behalf."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Apostate
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5712 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abu Walad Al-Hannat who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who apostatized from Islam. To whom does his inheritance go?
Imam (as) said: "His inheritance is divided among his heirs according to the Book of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic."
records.arabicText: 5712 - رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ أَبِي وَلاَّدٍ اَلْحَنَّاطِ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ اِرْتَدَّ عَنِ اَلْإِسْلاَمِ لِمَنْ يَكُونُ مِيرَاثُهُ قَالَ "يُقْسَمُ مِيرَاثُهُ عَلَى وَرَثَتِهِ عَلَى كِتَابِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5712 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abu Walad Al-Hannat who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who apostatized from Islam. To whom does his inheritance go?
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "His inheritance is divided among his heirs according to the Book of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Apostate
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5713 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Sayf ibn Umayrah from Abu Bakr Al-Hadrami from Abu Abdullah (as) who said: "If a Muslim man apostatizes from Islam, his wife becomes separated from him just as a woman becomes separated after a third divorce, and she must observe the waiting period (iddah) as a divorced woman does.
If he returns to Islam and repents before she marries another man, he may propose to her again, and she is not required to observe any waiting period for him, though she must observe it for anyone else.
However, if he is killed or dies before the completion of her waiting period, she must observe the waiting period of a widow (iddah of a deceased husband), and during that period, she inherits from him. But if she dies while he is still an apostate, he does not inherit from her."
records.arabicText: 5713 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ سَيْفِ بْنِ عَمِيرَةَ عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرٍ اَلْحَضْرَمِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "إِذَا اِرْتَدَّ اَلرَّجُلُ اَلْمُسْلِمُ عَنِ اَلْإِسْلاَمِ بَانَتْ مِنْهُ اِمْرَأَتُهُ كَمَا تَبِينُ اَلْمُطَلَّقَةُ ثَلاَثاً وَ تَعْتَدُّ مِنْهُ كَمَا تَعْتَدُّ اَلْمُطَلَّقَةُ فَإِنْ رَجَعَ إِلَى اَلْإِسْلاَمِ وَ تَابَ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَتَزَوَّجَ فَهُوَ خَاطِبٌ وَ لاَ عِدَّةَ عَلَيْهَا لَهُ وَ إِنَّمَا عَلَيْهَا اَلْعِدَّةُ لِغَيْرِهِ فَإِنْ قُتِلَ أَوْ مَاتَ قَبْلَ اِنْقِضَاءِ اَلْعِدَّةِ اِعْتَدَّتْ مِنْهُ عِدَّةَ اَلْمُتَوَفَّى عَنْهَا زَوْجُهَا فَهِيَ تَرِثُهُ فِي اَلْعِدَّةِ وَ لاَ يَرِثُهَا إِنْ مَاتَتْ وَ هُوَ مُرْتَدٌّ عَنِ اَلْإِسْلاَمِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5713 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Sayf ibn Umayrah from Abu Bakr Al-Hadrami from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"If a Muslim man apostatizes from Islam, his wife becomes separated from him just as a woman becomes separated after a third divorce, and she must observe the waiting period (iddah) as a divorced woman does.
If he returns to Islam and repents before she marries another man, he may propose to her again, and she is not required to observe any waiting period for him, though she must observe it for anyone else.
However, if he is killed or dies before the completion of her waiting period, she must observe the waiting period of a widow (iddah of a deceased husband), and during that period, she inherits from him. But if she dies while he is still an apostate, he does not inherit from her."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of One Who Has No Heir
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5714 - Al-Alaa narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
"Whoever dies and has no heir from relatives nor a freed slave who has guaranteed his liability (jarirah), then his wealth belongs to the anfal (public treasury or property of the Islamic state)."
records.arabicText: 5714 - رَوَى اَلْعَلاَءُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "مَنْ مَاتَ وَ لَيْسَ لَهُ وَارِثٌ مِنْ قَرَابَةٍ وَ لاَ مَوْلَى عَتَاقَةٍ قَدْ ضَمِنَ جَرِيرَتَهُ فَمَالُهُ مِنَ اَلْأَنْفَالِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5714 - Al-Alaa narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever dies and has no heir from relatives nor a freed slave who has guaranteed his liability (jarirah), then his wealth belongs to the anfal (public treasury or property of the Islamic state)."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of One Who Has No Heir
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5715 - It has also been narrated in another report: "Whoever dies and has no heir, his wealth belongs to his Hamshahrij," meaning the people of his town.
records.arabicText: 5715 - وَ قَدْ رُوِيَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ : "أَنَّ مَنْ مَاتَ وَ لَيْسَ لَهُ وَارِثٌ فَمَالُهُ لِهَمْشَهْرِيجِهِ " يَعْنِي أَهْلَ بَلَدِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5715 - It has also been narrated in another report:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever dies and has no heir, his wealth belongs to his Hamshahrij," meaning the people of his town.

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of One Who Has No Heir
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5716 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Malik ibn Atiyyah from Sulayman ibn Khalid from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a Muslim man who was killed and had a Christian father.
Imam (as) was asked: "To whom does his blood money (diyyah) go?"
Imam (as) said: "It is taken and placed in the treasury (Bayt Al-Mal) of the Muslims because his liability falls upon the treasury of the Muslims."
records.arabicText: 5716 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ مَالِكِ بْنِ عَطِيَّةَ عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ مُسْلِمٍ قُتِلَ وَ لَهُ أَبٌ نَصْرَانِيٌّ لِمَنْ تَكُونُ دِيَتُهُ قَالَ "تُؤْخَذُ فَتُجْعَلُ فِي بَيْتِ مَالِ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ لِأَنَّ جِنَايَتَهُ عَلَى بَيْتِ مَالِ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5716 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Malik ibn Atiyyah from Sulayman ibn Khalid from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a Muslim man who was killed and had a Christian father.
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) was asked: "To whom does his blood money (diyyah) go?"
Imam (as) said: "It is taken and placed in the treasury (Bayt Al-Mal) of the Muslims because his liability falls upon the treasury of the Muslims."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Followers of Different Religions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
"People of two different religions do not inherit from one another.
A Muslim inherits from a disbeliever, but a disbeliever does not inherit from a Muslim. This is because the fundamental ruling regarding the wealth of polytheists is that it becomes spoils (fay') for the Muslims, and Muslims have more right to it than the polytheists.
Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, has forbidden inheritance for disbelievers as a punishment for their disbelief, just as it is forbidden for a murderer to inherit as a punishment for their crime, then for what sin or punishment should a Muslim be deprived of inheritance? How could Islam bring them harm when the Prophet (sw) said: 'Islam elevates and does not degrade'?"
records.arabicText: لَا يَتَوَارَثُ أَهْلُ مِلَّتَيْنِ‌ وَ الْمُسْلِمُ يَرِثُ الْكَافِرَ وَ الْكَافِرُ لَا يَرِثُ الْمُسْلِمَ وَ ذَلِكَ أَنَّ أَصْلَ الْحُكْمِ فِي أَمْوَالِ الْمُشْرِكِينَ أَنَّهَا فَيْ‌ءٌ لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ وَ أَنَّ الْمُسْلِمِينَ أَحَقُّ بِهَا مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ وَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِنَّمَا حَرَّمَ عَلَى الْكُفَّارِ الْمِيرَاثَ عُقُوبَةً لَهُمْ بِكُفْرِهِمْ كَمَا حَرَّمَ عَلَى الْقَاتِلِ عُقُوبَةً لِقَتْلِهِ فَأَمَّا الْمُسْلِمُ فَلِأَيِّ جُرْمٍ وَ عُقُوبَةٍ يُحْرَمُ الْمِيرَاثَ وَ كَيْفَ صَارَ الْإِسْلَامُ يَزِيدُهُ شَرّاً مَعَ قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ ص.

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Followers of Different Religions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5717 - With the statement of the Prophet (sw), peace and blessings be upon him and his family: "Islam increases and does not decrease."
records.arabicText: 5717 - مَعَ قَوْلِ اَلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : " اَلْإِسْلاَمُ يَزِيدُ وَ لاَ يَنْقُصُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5717 - With the statement of the Prophet (sw), peace and blessings be upon him and his family:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Islam increases and does not decrease."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Followers of Different Religions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5718 - With the statement of the Prophet (sw), peace and blessings be upon him and his family: "There is no harm and no reciprocation of harm in Islam."
records.arabicText: 5718 - وَ مَعَ قَوْلِهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: "لاَ ضَرَرَ وَ لاَ إِضْرَارَ فِي اَلْإِسْلاَمِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5718 - With the statement of the Prophet (sw), peace and blessings be upon him and his family:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm and no reciprocation of harm in Islam."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Followers of Different Religions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5719 - With the statement of the Him (as), peace be upon him and his family:
"Islam prevails and is not prevailed over."
records.arabicText: 5719 - وَ مَعَ قَوْلِهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: " اَلْإِسْلاَمُ يَعْلُو وَ لاَ يُعْلَى عَلَيْهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5719 - With the statement of the Him (as), peace be upon him and his family:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Islam prevails and is not prevailed over."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Followers of Different Religions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5720 - It was narrated from Abu Al-Aswad Al-Duʾali that Muadh ibn Jabal was in Yemen when people gathered around him and said:
"A Jewish man has died and left behind a Muslim brother."
Muadh said: "I heard the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) say:
'Islam increases and does not diminish.'"
Therefore, he allowed the Muslim to inherit from his Jewish brother.
records.arabicText: 5720 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْأَسْوَدِ اَلدُّؤَلِيِّ : أَنَّ مُعَاذَ بْنَ جَبَلٍ كَانَ بِالْيَمَنِ فَاجْتَمَعُوا إِلَيْهِ وَ قَالُوا يَهُودِيٌّ مَاتَ وَ تَرَكَ أَخاً مُسْلِماً فَقَالَ مُعَاذٌ سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ يَقُولُ: " اَلْإِسْلاَمُ يَزِيدُ وَ لاَ يَنْقُصُ فَوَرَّثَ اَلْمُسْلِمَ مِنْ أَخِيهِ اَلْيَهُودِيِّ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5720 - It was narrated from Abu Al-Aswad Al-Duʾali that Muadh ibn Jabal was in Yemen when people gathered around him and said:
"A Jewish man has died and left behind a Muslim brother."
Muadh said: "I heard the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) say:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
'Islam increases and does not diminish.'"
Therefore, he allowed the Muslim to inherit from his Jewish brother.

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Followers of Different Religions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5721 - Muhammad ibn Sinan narrated from Abdur Rahman ibn Aʿyan from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) regarding a Christian man who dies leaving behind a Muslim son.
Imam (as) said: "Indeed, Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, has only increased us in honor through Islam. Therefore, we inherit from them, but they do not inherit from us."
records.arabicText: 5721 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سِنَانٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَعْيَنَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلنَّصْرَانِيِّ يَمُوتُ وَ لَهُ اِبْنٌ مُسْلِمٌ قَالَ "إِنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَمْ يَزِدْنَا بِالْإِسْلاَمِ إِلاَّ عِزّاً فَنَحْنُ نَرِثُهُمْ وَ لاَ يَرِثُونَّا".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5721 - Muhammad ibn Sinan narrated from Abdur Rahman ibn Aʿyan from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) regarding a Christian man who dies leaving behind a Muslim son.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, has only increased us in honor through Islam. Therefore, we inherit from them, but they do not inherit from us."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Followers of Different Religions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5722 - Zurah narrated from Samaah from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
I asked Imam (as) whether a Muslim can inherit from a polytheist.
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, but a polytheist does not inherit from a Muslim."
records.arabicText: 5722 - وَ رَوَى زُرْعَةُ عَنْ سَمَاعَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْمُسْلِمِ هَلْ يَرِثُ اَلْمُشْرِكَ فَقَالَ "نَعَمْ فَأَمَّا اَلْمُشْرِكُ فَلاَ يَرِثُ اَلْمُسْلِمَ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5722 - Zurah narrated from Samaah from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Imam (as) whether a Muslim can inherit from a polytheist.
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, but a polytheist does not inherit from a Muslim."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Followers of Different Religions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5723 - Musa ibn Bakr narrated from Abdur Rahman ibn Aʿyan from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"People of two different religions do not inherit from one another. We inherit from them, but they do not inherit from us.
Indeed, Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, has only increased us in honor through Islam."
records.arabicText: 5723 - وَ رَوَى مُوسَى بْنُ بَكْرٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَعْيَنَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "لاَ يَتَوَارَثُ أَهْلُ مِلَّتَيْنِ نَحْنُ نَرِثُهُمْ وَ لاَ يَرِثُونَّا فَإِنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَمْ يَزِدْنَا، بِالْإِسْلاَمِ إِلاَّ عِزّاً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5723 - Musa ibn Bakr narrated from Abdur Rahman ibn Aʿyan from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"People of two different religions do not inherit from one another. We inherit from them, but they do not inherit from us.
Indeed, Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, has only increased us in honor through Islam."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Followers of Different Religions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5724 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Al-Hasan ibn Salih from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"A Muslim prevents a disbeliever from inheriting and inherits from him, but a disbeliever neither prevents a believer from inheriting nor does he inherit from him."
records.arabicText: 5724 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنِ اَلْحَسَنِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : " اَلْمُسْلِمُ يَحْجُبُ اَلْكَافِرَ وَ يَرِثُهُ وَ اَلْكَافِرُ لاَ يَحْجُبُ اَلْمُؤْمِنَ وَ لاَ يَرِثُهُ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5724 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Al-Hasan ibn Salih from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A Muslim prevents a disbeliever from inheriting and inherits from him, but a disbeliever neither prevents a believer from inheriting nor does he inherit from him."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Followers of Different Religions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5725 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abu Walad Al-Hannat who said:
I heard Abu Abdullah (as) say: "A Muslim man inherits from his dhimmi (non-Muslim under Islamic protection) wife, but she does not inherit from him."
records.arabicText:
5725 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ أَبِي وَلاَّدٍ اَلْحَنَّاطِ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ :
" اَلْمُسْلِمُ يَرِثُ اِمْرَأَتَهُ اَلذِّمِّيَّةَ وَ هِيَ لاَ تَرِثُهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5725 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abu Walad Al-Hannat who said:
I heard Abu Abdullah (as) say:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A Muslim man inherits from his dhimmi (non-Muslim under Islamic protection) wife, but she does not inherit from him."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Followers of Different Religions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5726 - Al-Hasan ibn Ali Al-Khazzaz narrated from Ahmad ibn A'id from Abu Khadijah from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"A disbeliever does not inherit from a Muslim, but a Muslim may inherit from a disbeliever - unless the Muslim has made a will (wasiyyah) bequeathing something to the disbeliever."
records.arabicText: 5726 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ اَلْخَزَّازُ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ عَائِذٍ عَنْ أَبِي خَدِيجَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "لاَ يَرِثُ اَلْكَافِرُ اَلْمُسْلِمَ وَ لِلْمُسْلِمِ أَنْ يَرِثَ اَلْكَافِرَ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ اَلْمُسْلِمُ قَدْ أَوْصَى لِلْكَافِرِ بِشَيْءٍ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5726 - Al-Hasan ibn Ali Al-Khazzaz narrated from Ahmad ibn A'id from Abu Khadijah from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A disbeliever does not inherit from a Muslim, but a Muslim may inherit from a disbeliever - unless the Muslim has made a will (wasiyyah) bequeathing something to the disbeliever."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Followers of Different Religions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5727 - Asim ibn Humaid narrated from Muhammad ibn Qays who said:
I heard Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) say: "A Jew and a Christian do not inherit from Muslims, but Muslims inherit from Jews and Christians."
records.arabicText: 5727 - وَ رَوَى عَاصِمُ بْنُ حُمَيْدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ قَيْسٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ : "لاَ يَرِثُ اَلْيَهُودِيُّ وَ اَلنَّصْرَانِيُّ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ وَ يَرِثُ اَلْمُسْلِمُونَ اَلْيَهُودِيَّ وَ اَلنَّصْرَانِيَّ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5727 - Asim ibn Humaid narrated from Muhammad ibn Qays who said:
I heard Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) say:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A Jew and a Christian do not inherit from Muslims, but Muslims inherit from Jews and Christians."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Followers of Different Religions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5728 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Ali ibn Ri'ab from Abu Basir who said:
I asked Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a Muslim man who died and had a Christian mother, along with a Muslim wife and Muslim children.
Imam (as) said: "If his mother accepts Islam before the inheritance is divided, she is given one-sixth."
I asked: "What if he has no wife, no children, and no heir entitled to a share in the Book from among the Muslims, and his mother is Christian, and his relatives are Christians who would have a share in the Book if they were Muslim - who inherits his wealth?"
Imam (as) said: "If his mother embraces Islam, then all of his inheritance belongs to her. But if his mother does not embrace Islam and some of his relatives who have a rightful share in the Book accept Islam, the inheritance goes to them. If none of his relatives become Muslim, then his inheritance belongs to the Imam."
records.arabicText: 5728 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ رِئَابٍ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ مُسْلِمٍ مَاتَ وَ لَهُ أُمٌّ نَصْرَانِيَّةٌ وَ لَهُ زَوْجَةٌ وَ وُلْدٌ مُسْلِمُونَ فَقَالَ "إِنْ أَسْلَمَتْ أُمُّهُ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُقْسَمَ مِيرَاثُهُ أُعْطِيَتِ اَلسُّدُسَ" قُلْتُ فَإِنْ لَمْ تَكُنْ لَهُ اِمْرَأَةٌ وَ لاَ وَلَدٌ وَ لاَ وَارِثٌ لَهُ سَهْمٌ فِي اَلْكِتَابِ مِنَ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ وَ أُمُّهُ نَصْرَانِيَّةٌ وَ قَرَابَتُهُ نَصَارَى مِمَّنْ لَهُمْ سَهْمٌ فِي اَلْكِتَابِ لَوْ كَانُوا مُسْلِمِينَ لِمَنْ يَكُونُ مِيرَاثُهُ قَالَ "إِنْ أَسْلَمَتْ أُمُّهُ فَإِنَّ جَمِيعَ مِيرَاثِهِ لَهَا وَ إِنْ لَمْ تُسْلِمْ أُمُّهُ وَ أَسْلَمَ بَعْضُ قَرَابَتِهِ مِمَّنْ لَهُ سَهْمٌ فِي اَلْكِتَابِ فَإِنَّ مِيرَاثَهُ لَهُ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يُسْلِمْ مِنْ قَرَابَتِهِ أَحَدٌ فَإِنَّ مِيرَاثَهُ لِلْإِمَامِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5728 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Ali ibn Ri'ab from Abu Basir who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a Muslim man who died and had a Christian mother, along with a Muslim wife and Muslim children.
Imam (as) said: "If his mother accepts Islam before the inheritance is divided, she is given one-sixth."
I asked: "What if he has no wife, no children, and no heir entitled to a share in the Book from among the Muslims, and his mother is Christian, and his relatives are Christians who would have a share in the Book if they were Muslim - who inherits his wealth?"
Imam (as) said: "If his mother embraces Islam, then all of his inheritance belongs to her. But if his mother does not embrace Islam and some of his relatives who have a rightful share in the Book accept Islam, the inheritance goes to them. If none of his relatives become Muslim, then his inheritance belongs to the Imam."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Followers of Different Religions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5729 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Hisham ibn Salim from Abd Al-Malik ibn Aʿyan or Malik ibn Aʿyan from Abu Jafar (as) who said: I asked Imam (as) about a Christian man who died, leaving behind a Muslim nephew (son of his brother) and a Muslim niece (daughter of his sister), while his own children and wife were Christians.
Imam (as) said: "I see that the Muslim nephew should be given two-thirds of what he left behind, and the Muslim niece should be given one-third of what he left behind, provided he does not have young children. However, if he has young children, then the two heirs must provide for the children from what they inherited from their father until the children reach maturity."
It was asked: "How should they provide for the children?"
Imam (as) replied: "The heir of the two-thirds share must cover two-thirds of the expenses, and the heir of the one-third share must cover one-third of the expenses. Once the children mature, the heirs are no longer responsible for their expenses."
It was then asked: "What if his children embrace Islam while they are still young?"
Imam (as) said: "In that case, what their father left behind should be handed over to the Imam until they reach maturity. If they remain steadfast in Islam upon reaching maturity, the Imam will give them their inheritance. But if they do not remain in Islam upon maturity, the Imam will return the inheritance to the Muslim nephew and niece, giving the nephew two-thirds of what was left and the niece one-third."
records.arabicText: 5729 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلْمَلِكِ بْنِ أَعْيَنَ أَوْ مَالِكِ بْنِ أَعْيَنَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ نَصْرَانِيٍّ مَاتَ وَ لَهُ اِبْنُ أَخٍ مُسْلِمٌ وَ اِبْنُ أُخْتٍ مُسْلِمٌ وَ لِلنَّصْرَانِيِّ أَوْلاَدٌ وَ زَوْجَةٌ نَصَارَى فَقَالَ "أَرَى أَنْ يُعْطَى اِبْنُ أَخِيهِ اَلْمُسْلِمُ ثُلُثَيْ مَا تَرَكَ وَ يُعْطَى اِبْنُ أُخْتِهِ اَلْمُسْلِمُ ثُلُثَ مَا تَرَكَ إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ وُلْدٌ صِغَارٌ فَإِنْ كَانَ لَهُ وُلْدٌ صِغَارٌ فَإِنَّ عَلَى اَلْوَارِثَيْنِ أَنْ يُنْفِقَا عَلَى اَلصِّغَارِ مِمَّا وَرِثَا عَنْ أَبِيهِمْ حَتَّى يُدْرِكُوا" قِيلَ لَهُ كَيْفَ يُنْفِقَانِ عَلَى اَلصِّغَارِ فَقَالَ "يُخْرِجُ وَارِثُ اَلثُّلُثَيْنِ ثُلُثَيِ اَلنَّفَقَةِ وَ يُخْرِجُ وَارِثُ اَلثُّلُثِ ثُلُثَ اَلنَّفَقَةِ فَإِذَا أَدْرَكُوا قَطَعُوا اَلنَّفَقَةَ عَنْهُمْ" قِيلَ لَهُ فَإِنْ أَسْلَمَ أَوْلاَدُهُ وَ هُمْ صِغَارٌ فَقَالَ "يُدْفَعُ مَا تَرَكَ أَبُوهُمْ إِلَى اَلْإِمَامِ حَتَّى يُدْرِكُوا فَإِنْ أَتَمُّوا عَلَى اَلْإِسْلاَمِ إِذَا أَدْرَكُوا دَفَعَ اَلْإِمَامُ مِيرَاثَهُ إِلَيْهِمْ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يُتِمُّوا عَلَى اَلْإِسْلاَمِ إِذَا أَدْرَكُوا دَفَعَ اَلْإِمَامُ مِيرَاثَهُ إِلَى اِبْنِ أَخِيهِ وَ إِلَى اِبْنِ أُخْتِهِ اَلْمُسْلِمَيْنِ يَدْفَعُ إِلَى اِبْنِ أَخِيهِ ثُلُثَيْ مَا تَرَكَ وَ يَدْفَعُ إِلَى اِبْنِ أُخْتِهِ ثُلُثَ مَا تَرَكَ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5729 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Hisham ibn Salim from Abd Al-Malik ibn Aʿyan or Malik ibn Aʿyan from Abu Jafar (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Imam (as) about a Christian man who died, leaving behind a Muslim nephew (son of his brother) and a Muslim niece (daughter of his sister), while his own children and wife were Christians.
Imam (as) said: "I see that the Muslim nephew should be given two-thirds of what he left behind, and the Muslim niece should be given one-third of what he left behind, provided he does not have young children. However, if he has young children, then the two heirs must provide for the children from what they inherited from their father until the children reach maturity."
It was asked: "How should they provide for the children?"
Imam (as) replied: "The heir of the two-thirds share must cover two-thirds of the expenses, and the heir of the one-third share must cover one-third of the expenses. Once the children mature, the heirs are no longer responsible for their expenses."
It was then asked: "What if his children embrace Islam while they are still young?"
Imam (as) said: "In that case, what their father left behind should be handed over to the Imam until they reach maturity. If they remain steadfast in Islam upon reaching maturity, the Imam will give them their inheritance. But if they do not remain in Islam upon maturity, the Imam will return the inheritance to the Muslim nephew and niece, giving the nephew two-thirds of what was left and the niece one-third."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Followers of Different Religions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5730 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Ibrahim ibn Abd Al-Hamid who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a Christian man who accepted Islam and then reverted to Christianity and died.
Imam (as) said: "His inheritance goes to his Christian children."
I then asked about a Muslim who converted to Christianity and then died.
Imam (as) said: "His inheritance goes to his Muslim children."
records.arabicText: 5730 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اَلْحَمِيدِ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ نَصْرَانِيٌّ أَسْلَمَ ثُمَّ رَجَعَ إِلَى اَلنَّصْرَانِيَّةِ ثُمَّ مَاتَ قَالَ "مِيرَاثُهُ لِوُلْدِهِ اَلنَّصَارَى " وَ مُسْلِمٌ تَنَصَّرَ ثُمَّ مَاتَ قَالَ "مِيرَاثُهُ لِوُلْدِهِ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5730 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Ibrahim ibn Abd Al-Hamid who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a Christian man who accepted Islam and then reverted to Christianity and died.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"His inheritance goes to his Christian children."
I then asked about a Muslim who converted to Christianity and then died.
Imam (as) said: "His inheritance goes to his Muslim children."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of Slaves
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5732 - Hanan ibn Sadir narrated from Ibn Abi Yafur from Ishaq ibn Ammar from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"A servant of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) passed away.
Imam Ali (as) said: 'Look and see if he has any heirs.'
It was said to Him (as): 'He has two daughters in Yamamah who are enslaved.'
So, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) purchased them using the deceased's wealth and then gave them the remainder of the inheritance."
records.arabicText:
5732 - وَ رَوَى حَنَانُ بْنُ سَدِيرٍ عَنِ اِبْنِ أَبِي يَعْفُورٍ عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "مَاتَ مَوْلًى لِعَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَقَالَ
"اُنْظُرُوا هَلْ تَجِدُونَ لَهُ وَارِثاً" فَقِيلَ لَهُ إِنَّ لَهُ اِبْنَتَيْنِ بِالْيَمَامَةِ مَمْلُوكَتَيْنِ فَاشْتَرَاهُمَا مِنْ مَالِ اَلْمَيِّتِ ثُمَّ دَفَعَ إِلَيْهِمَا بَقِيَّةَ اَلْمِيرَاثِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5732 - Hanan ibn Sadir narrated from Ibn Abi Yafur from Ishaq ibn Ammar from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"A servant of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) passed away.
Imam Ali (as) said: 'Look and see if he has any heirs.'
It was said to Him (as): 'He has two daughters in Yamamah who are enslaved.'
So, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) purchased them using the deceased's wealth and then gave them the remainder of the inheritance."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of Slaves
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5731 - Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said that Commander of the Faithful (as) used to say regarding a free man who dies while his mother is a slave:
"She should be purchased from the wealth of her son, then freed, and then she will inherit from him."
records.arabicText: 5731 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ كَانَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ : فِي اَلرَّجُلِ اَلْحُرِّ يَمُوتُ وَ لَهُ أُمٌّ مَمْلُوكَةٌ قَالَ "تُشْتَرَى مِنْ مَالِ اِبْنِهَا ثُمَّ تُعْتَقُ ثُمَّ يُوَرَّثُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5731 - Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said that Commander of the Faithful (as) used to say regarding a free man who dies while his mother is a slave:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "She should be purchased from the wealth of her son, then freed, and then she will inherit from him."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Inheritance of Slaves
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5733 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Jameel who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man who dies and leaves behind a son who is enslaved.
Imam (as) said: "His son should be purchased from his wealth, then freed, and he will inherit whatever remains."
records.arabicText:
5733 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ جَمِيلٍ قَالَ :
سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَمُوتُ وَ يَتْرُكُ اِبْناً مَمْلُوكاً قَالَ "يُشْتَرَى اِبْنُهُ مِنْ مَالِهِ فَيُعْتَقُ وَ يُوَرَّثُ مَا بَقِيَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5733 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Jameel who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man who dies and leaves behind a son who is enslaved.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "His son should be purchased from his wealth, then freed, and he will inherit whatever remains."
